steps.title: Identify the phrase structure
steps.content: The blank requires a noun that serves as part of the subject. That noun “economists” is directly followed by an appositive — “Laura Gee and Muriel Niederle” — a phrase that renames or identifies the noun.

steps.title: Apply the rule for essential appositives
steps.content: The names identify which behavioral economists are the subject of the sentence, making the appositive essential. Appositives that are essential to the meaning are not set off by commas. Option B correctly presents the noun and appositive together without punctuation: “economists Laura Gee and Muriel Niederle.” Options A and D incorrectly introduce commas around or after the appositive, while C adds a comma within it. That matches choice B — B is correct.
